theory.canonical_statement: The inter-modal transfer rate \(T_{\mathrm{IM}}\) between two modes \(m_1, m_2\) of a field \(\phi\) is defined by \[ T_{\mathrm{IM}} \;=\; \frac{1}{u_T} \int_{\Sigma} \phi_{m_1}^* \, \mathcal{L} \, \phi_{m_2} \, \mathrm{d}\Sigma, \] where \(\mathcal{L}\) is the Trawinistic Lagrangian density and \(\Sigma\) is a hypersurface away from the TZP.\\
theory.technical_interpretation: Technically, Inter-Modal Transfer Rate is interpreted through the variables and operators present in the canonical relation. The equation support section should preserve these source equations in sequence so later proof and Wolfram passes operate on the same mathematical object.

equation_progression.latex: The inter-modal transfer rate \(T_{\mathrm{IM}}\) between two modes \(m_1, m_2\) of a field \(\phi\) is defined by \[ T_{\mathrm{IM}} \;=\; \frac{1}{u_T} \int_{\Sigma} \phi_{m_1}^* \, \mathcal{L} \, \phi_{m_2} \, \mathrm{d}\Sigma, \] where \(\mathcal{L}\) is the Trawinistic Lagrangian density and \(\Sigma\) is a hypersurface away from the TZP.\\

$\KNL$ is a dimensionless constant defined by the ratio $\KNL = \frac{\lVert \Nabla^2 \PhiField \rVert}{\lVert \Nabla \PhiField \rVert^2}$ evaluated in the limit as the field $\PhiField$ approaches the TZP, with $\Nabla$ denoting the Trawinistic connection.\\
